exercises.title: User Manual of Me
exercises.description: Create your own 'user manual' — a one-page document describing how to work with you. Then have your team do the same and share.
hints: Include: communication preferences, best working hours, pet peeves, strengths
Be honest — the more authentic, the more useful
Share in a team session and discuss how to leverage differences

solution.code:
User Manual of Me Template:

About Me:
- I do my best work when: ___________
- My communication style: [direct/diplomatic, async/prefer calls]
- My strengths: ___________
- What frustrates me: ___________
- How to give me feedback: ___________
- My working hours: ___________
- How I handle stress: ___________
- What I need from my team: ___________

Example:
- I do my best work in the morning — schedule important meetings before noon
- Communication: I prefer Slack for quick questions, email for complex topics
- Strengths: breaking down complex problems, mentoring
- Frustrations: meetings without agendas, last-minute changes
- Feedback: direct, please. Don't sugarcoat.
- Working hours: 8am-5pm, but flexible
- Stress: I need 30 minutes of quiet time after a tough meeting
- Need: trust and autonomy to do my best work

exercises.title: Team Retrospective Facilitation
exercises.description: Facilitate a team retrospective using the Start/Stop/Continue framework. Capture actions and follow up next week.
hints: Create psychological safety — no blame, focus on the process
Limit to 3 action items — too many won't get done

solution.code:
Team Retrospective Agenda (60 min):

1. Set the stage (5 min):
   'This is a safe space. We're here to improve, not blame.'

2. Gather data (15 min):
   Each person writes sticky notes for:
   - Start: What should we START doing?
   - Stop: What should we STOP doing?
   - Continue: What should we CONTINUE doing?

3. Discuss (20 min):
   Group similar items. Discuss the top themes.
   For each: 'What specifically will we do?'

4. Decide actions (10 min):
   Limit to 3 actions. Each has:
   - What: ___________
   - Who: ___________
   - By when: ___________

5. Close (10 min):
   One-word check-out: How are you feeling?
   Schedule next retro.

exercises.title: Team Culture Ritual Design
exercises.description: Design three recurring rituals for your team: one weekly, one monthly, one quarterly. Each should serve a specific purpose.
hints: Start small — consistency matters more than grandeur
Make rituals inclusive of all personality types
Track whether rituals are actually happening and adjust

solution.code:
Team Culture Rituals Plan:

Weekly Ritual:
Name: ___________
Purpose: ___________
Duration: ___________
When: ___________
Example: Weekly Wins — every Friday, 15 min, each person shares one win

Monthly Ritual:
Name: ___________
Purpose: ___________
Duration: ___________
When: ___________
Example: Team Lunch — first Friday of month, informal, no work talk

Quarterly Ritual:
Name: ___________
Purpose: ___________
Duration: ___________
When: ___________
Example: Team Retrospective + Offsite — half day, reflect + plan

Success measures:
- Are people showing up? ___________
- Do they value it? (Ask)
- Is it changing behavior? ___________
- Adjust quarterly based on feedback
